---
layout: default
title: "National Political Brief: Election Commission Bypolls and Legislative Setbacks"
date: 2026-07-07T06:59:08.236532+00:00
summary: "The ECI announces Rajya Sabha bypolls for West Bengal while the Opposition prepares to challenge the government over legislative failures and election processes"
run_time_ist: "12:29PM"
---

<article class="digest-post">
  <a class="back-link" href="{{ '/' | relative_url }}">Political Brief</a>
  <p class="post-meta">Gemini Summary: 12:29PM</p>


<h2 class="section-title">India National Politics</h2>

<ul class="digest-points">
  <li><strong>Rajya Sabha Bypolls:</strong> The Election Commission has scheduled bypolls for three Rajya Sabha seats in West Bengal on July 24, a move that may require the commission to determine the official status of the Trinamool Congress.<span class="source-chips"><a href="https://news.google.?oc=5&amp;hl=en-US&amp;gl=US&amp;ceid=US%3Aen" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">I1</a> <a href="https://news.google.?oc=5&amp;hl=en-US&amp;gl=US&amp;ceid=US%3Aen" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">I2</a> <a href="https://news.google.?oc=5&amp;hl=en-US&amp;gl=US&amp;ceid=US%3Aen" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">I3</a></span></li>
  <li><strong>Legislative Setbacks:</strong> The Constitutional Amendment Bill aimed at providing women’s reservation in the Lok Sabha and State Assemblies has failed to pass in the Lok Sabha.<span class="source-chips"><a href="https://news.google.com/rss/articles/CBMiqwFBVV95cUxPSTVKRHVGUXlhNjVqNEczYkNpd1NvbmRYOU5Fd2RIZjV3OHBob0owSzh3LVRXdUVqSDNVZTgtbVZlUG8ybS1wallfSVdTeTRtNENIU0lwV3piczdxV2NEVmE0WC1GSTQyMjR4dkNPb1pqblB2SzNHQjZBRWVXZHpPZXE2bVlQc3NGNEFJVU1NUHJMMFFqSkdRUVFFMGZNRUkzSTc0LXphM1JaLXM?oc=5&amp;hl=en-US&amp;gl=US&amp;ceid=US%3Aen" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">I4</a></span></li>
  <li><strong>Opposition Strategy:</strong> The INDIA bloc has raised concerns with the Chief Justice of India regarding the Election Commission’s SIR process, while the Opposition plans to target the government over paper leaks and donation issues during the upcoming monsoon session.<span class="source-chips"><a href="https://news.google.com/rss/articles/CBMiqgFBVV95cUxNaTYyZUtBaFRlX2hPNnJNd1did1ZaM21EVElkZWRaY1lSbHByUURNS29pbjZaMFZFcl93WUFRSE8tSE53YkhaOHlMYjZqQWlERC11Z2JvQU1najVPaVpnWldRSTlsLTloVWktMG9OQU9VcmFFSGJHc1lmdnZQSHVqQ2ptRkF3OTRTYnd2TkRtZmJDX1hJT2pVYmN5X2FaaVhDNVRlVWFfNjZEQQ?oc=5&amp;hl=en-US&amp;gl=US&amp;ceid=US%3Aen" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">I5</a> <a href="https://news.google.?oc=5&amp;hl=en-US&amp;gl=US&amp;ceid=US%3Aen" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">I6</a></span></li>
</ul>

<h2 class="section-title">Policy and Institutions</h2>

<ul class="digest-points">
  <li><strong>Waqf Board Controversy:</strong> The Congress party is planning to move the Supreme Court to challenge the recent reorganization of the Madhya Pradesh Waqf Board.<span class="source-chips"><a href="https://news.google.?oc=5&amp;hl=en-US&amp;gl=US&amp;ceid=US%3Aen" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">W1</a> <a href="https://news.google.?oc=5&amp;hl=en-US&amp;gl=US&amp;ceid=US%3Aen" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">W8</a></span></li>
</ul>
